info.title: Modjo Public API
info.description:
The Modjo Public API lets you programmatically access your Modjo workspace data — calls, users, teams, and webhooks.

### Base URL

```
https://api.modjo.ai//v2
```

### Getting Started

1. [Create an API key](#tag/Authentication) from your Modjo settings.
2. Include it as a Bearer token in the `Authorization` header of every request.
3. Browse the endpoints below to list calls, manage users, configure webhooks, and more.

### Pagination

All list endpoints return paginated results. Use the `page` (1-based, default: 1) and `size` (default: 25, max: 100) query parameters to navigate through results. Every paginated response includes a `pagination` object with `page`, `size`, and `total` fields.

### Expanding Relations

Some endpoints support a `expand` query parameter that lets you inline related objects instead of receiving only their IDs. Pass a comma-separated list of fields (e.g. `?expand=contacts,deal`). When a relation is expanded, the corresponding ID field is omitted from the response.

### Need Help?

Contact your Modjo account manager or reach out to [support@modjo.ai](mailto:support@modjo.ai).

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description:
Modjo API authentication is based on an API key that must be included in the request headers.

To authenticate, add your API key to the `Authorization` header as a Bearer token:

```
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY
```

### How to Retrieve Your API Key

Before generating a new API key, you must have **Administrator** or **Manager** permissions.

Follow these steps to create your API key:

1. **[Log in to Modjo](https://app.modjo.ai)**
2. **[Go to Settings](https://app.modjo.ai/settings/?utm_source=api-doc)**
3. **[Access the Integrations section](https://app.modjo.ai/settings/integrations?utm_source=api-doc)**
4. **[Find the Public API card](https://app.modjo.ai/settings/integrations/modjo_api_key?utm_source=api-doc)**
5. Create a new API key.

Make sure to keep your API key **secure** and do not share it with unauthorized parties.

### Rate Limiting

The API enforces a rate limit of **100 requests per minute** per API key. Rate limit information is included in response headers:

- `X-RateLimit-Limit`: Maximum number of requests allowed per window
- `X-RateLimit-Remaining`: Number of requests remaining in the current window
- `X-RateLimit-Reset`: Seconds until the rate limit resets

If you exceed the rate limit, you will receive a `429 Too Many Requests` response.

tags.name: Errors
tags.description:
The API uses conventional HTTP status codes and returns a consistent JSON error body.

### Error Response Format

```json
{
  "code": "not_found",
  "message": "Call not found"
}
```

### Error Codes

| HTTP Status | Code | Description |
|---|---|---|
| 400 | `validation_error` | The request body or query parameters are invalid. The `message` field describes which fields failed validation. |
| 401 | `missing_api_key` | No `Authorization` header was provided. |
| 401 | `invalid_api_key` | The API key is invalid, revoked, or the tenant has been deleted. |
| 404 | `not_found` | The requested resource does not exist. |
| 409 | `conflict` | The resource already exists (e.g. duplicate email or webhook name). |
| 429 | `rate_limited` | You have exceeded the rate limit. Wait and retry after the `X-RateLimit-Reset` interval. |
| 500 | `internal_error` | An unexpected error occurred on our side. If this persists, contact support. |

tags.name: Calls
tags.description:
A call represents a recorded conversation in Modjo (phone call, video meeting, etc.).

### Call Statuses

| Status | Description |
|---|---|
| `processing` | The call has been received and is being downloaded, transcribed, or analyzed. |
| `completed` | The call is fully processed and the transcript is available. |
| `failed` | Processing failed (e.g. empty recording, damaged file, no speakers detected). |

### Uploading Calls

Use `POST /calls` to upload a call via a publicly accessible recording URL. The call is processed asynchronously — you will receive a UUID immediately and can poll `GET /calls/{id}` until the status changes from `processing` to `completed` or `failed`. Typical processing time is 5 to 30 minutes.

### Expanding Relations

The list and get endpoints support the `expand` query parameter with the following fields:
- **contacts** — inline contact details instead of `contactIds`
- **deal** — inline deal details instead of `dealId`
- **account** — inline account details instead of `accountId`
- **users** — inline user details instead of `userIds`

tags.name: CRM Filling Answers
tags.description: CRM filling answers represent values that have been pushed to a CRM after a call was processed. Each answer is linked to a specific call and a CRM filling field. Only answers that have been successfully pushed to the CRM are returned.

tags.name: Contacts
tags.description: Contacts represent external people associated with your calls and deals (e.g. prospects, customers). Use these endpoints to list and search contacts in your workspace.

tags.name: CRM Filling Templates
tags.description: CRM filling templates define how Modjo automatically fills CRM fields after calls. Each template specifies the language, call duration filters, applicable providers, and which teams and tags it applies to. Templates can be in `pending` (draft) or `published` (active) status.

tags.name: Deals
tags.description: Deals represent sales opportunities tracked in your CRM. Use these endpoints to list deals and retrieve AI-generated deal summaries.

tags.name: Tags
tags.description: Tags are labels that can be applied to calls for categorization and filtering. Use this endpoint to list available tags in your workspace.

tags.name: Topics
tags.description: Topics represent conversation subjects detected in calls (e.g. pricing, competitors). Topics can be grouped into categories. Use this endpoint to list available topics in your workspace.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: Users represent licensed members of your Modjo workspace. Each user has a unique email address within the tenant. Use these endpoints to list, create, update, or deactivate users, and to manage their team membership programmatically.

tags.name: Teams
tags.description: Teams let you group users together for organizational purposes (e.g. "Sales", "Customer Success"). Use these endpoints to manage teams and their membership.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description:
Webhooks let you receive real-time notifications when events occur in your Modjo workspace.

### Supported Events

| Event | Description |
|---|---|
| `call_summarized` | Fired when a call has been fully processed and its summary is available. |
| `call_recording_deleted` | Fired when a call recording has been deleted. |
| `call_transcript_deleted` | Fired when a call transcript has been deleted. |

### Delivery Format

When an event fires, Modjo sends a `POST` request to your webhook URL with a JSON payload:

```json
{
  "event": "call_summarized",
  "timestamp": "2024-06-15T14:30:00.000Z",
  "data": {
    "callId": 12345
  }
}
```

### Security

Each webhook is assigned a secret token on creation. Modjo includes this token in the `X-Webhook-Secret` header of every delivery request so you can verify the request authenticity.

### Retry Policy

If your endpoint returns a non-2xx status code, Modjo will retry the delivery with exponential backoff.

bearerAuth.description: To retrieve your API key please refer to the [Authentication](#tag/Authentication) section of this documentation.

crmLink.description: URL to account in external CRM

code.description: Machine-readable error code (e.g. validation_error, not_found, conflict)

message.description: Human-readable error message with details about the failure
message.example: The requested resource was not found

tagId.description: ID of the tag to add to the call

data.description: List of AI-extracted next steps from the call. Empty if no next steps were detected or the call is still processing.

title.description: Short title of the next step

description.description: Detailed description of the next step
description.example: Set up a 30-minute follow-up call with the prospect to review the proposal by end of week.

rawContent.description: Note content as a rich-text JSON structure

modifiedOn.description: Last modification timestamp in ISO 8601 format

createdBy.description: ID of the user who created the note

modifiedBy.description: ID of the user who last modified the note

modifiedOn.description: Last modification timestamp in ISO 8601 format

phoneProviderCallId.description: External identifier of the call from the phone provider (e.g. Aircall, Ringover, etc.).

recordingRetentionStatus.description: Retention status of the call recording: "available" if the recording is kept, "deleted" if it has been deleted for data retention reasons.

transcriptRetentionStatus.description: Retention status of the call transcript: "available" if the transcript is kept, "deleted" if it has been deleted for data retention reasons.

contacts.description: Expanded contact objects. Present when expand=contacts is set.

contactIds.description: Contact IDs. Present when contacts are not expanded.

amount.description: Deal amount in the deal currency

closeDate.description: Expected close date in ISO 8601 format

deal.description: Expanded deal object. Present when expand=deal is set.

dealId.description: Deal ID. Present when deal is not expanded.

account.description: Expanded account object. Present when expand=account is set.

accountId.description: Account ID. Present when account is not expanded.

users.description: Expanded user objects. Present when expand=users is set.

userIds.description: User IDs. Present when users are not expanded.

crmId.description: Identifier of the activity record in the external CRM

crmOrigin.description: How the activity was logged in the CRM: created_by_modjo, found_in_crm, or provided_by_provider

CrmActivity.description: CRM activity record linked to this call. Null if the call has not been synced to a CRM.

startTime.description: Block start time in seconds from the beginning of the call

endTime.description: Block end time in seconds from the beginning of the call

content.description: Transcript text content for this block
content.example: Hi, thanks for joining the call today.

type.description: Speaker type: user (internal team member), contact (external participant), or unknown

callId.description: ID of the call this answer belongs to

crmFillingFieldUuid.description: UUID of the CRM filling field this answer is for

value.description: Value that was pushed to the CRM. Type depends on the CRM field type.

crmId.description: CRM record ID this answer was pushed to

templateUuid.description: UUID of the summary template used to generate this summary

templateLength.description: Length setting of the template: short, detailed, or adjusted (adapts to call duration)

answer.description: The AI-generated summary text. Null if the summary has not been generated yet.
answer.example: The team discussed Q3 targets and agreed to increase outbound outreach by 20%.

callId.description: UUID of the created call. Use with GET /calls/{id} to poll for processing status.

status.description: Call is queued for async processing

downloadMediaUrl.description: Publicly accessible recording URL. Modjo will download the file from this URL.

name.description: Call title. If omitted, auto-generated from participant names.

date.description: Call start datetime in ISO 8601 format

duration.description: Call duration in seconds. Can be 0 or omitted.

type.description: Participant type: "user" for internal team members, "contact" for external participants

participants.description: List of call participants. At least one participant of type "user" must map to a licensed user

tags.description: Tag names to attach to the call

account.description: Link the call to a CRM account directly

deal.description: Link the call to a CRM deal directly

crmLink.description: URL to contact in external CRM

status.description: Template status. `pending` means in draft, `published` means active.

providers.description: List of call providers this template applies to

teamIds.description: IDs of teams this template is assigned to

tagIds.description: IDs of tags this template is filtered by

order.description: Display order of the field within the template

prompt.description: AI prompt used to extract the field value from the call
prompt.example: What was the next step agreed upon?

entityType.description: CRM entity type this field maps to

fieldType.description: Data type of the CRM field

isActive.description: Whether the field is currently active

isAutoPush.description: Whether the field value is automatically pushed to the CRM

crmLink.description: URL to deal in external CRM

amount.description: Deal amount in the deal currency

value.example: The prospect is interested in the enterprise plan.

modifiedOn.description: Last modification timestamp in ISO 8601 format

role.description: User role: user, manager, or admin

modifiedOn.description: Last modification timestamp in ISO 8601 format

saidBy.description: Filter for who said the topic

role.description: User role: user, manager, or admin

modifiedOn.description: Last modification timestamp in ISO 8601 format

teamId.description: ID of the team to add the user to

modifiedOn.description: Last modification timestamp in ISO 8601 format

name.description: Webhook name. Must be unique within the tenant.

url.description: HTTPS URL where event payloads will be delivered

events.description: List of event names to subscribe to. At least one event is required.

name.description: Webhook name. Must be unique within the tenant.

url.description: HTTPS URL where event payloads will be delivered

events.description: List of event names to subscribe to. At least one event is required.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of accounts. Supports filtering by account name.
Returns a single account by ID.

post.summary: Add a tag to a call
post.description: Adds a tag to a call. If the tag is already associated with the call, the operation is idempotent and returns the existing association.
